assertions.description: INCORRECT: The schematic places #FLG01 (PWR_FLAG) at (146.05, 78.74) whose power_out pin is wired to the same node as #PWR02 (GNDREF at 147.32, 78.74) via the wire segment (146.05, 78.74)→(147.32, 78.74). This makes GNDREF a PWR_FLAG-sourced net. However, the analyzer emits a pwr_flag_warnings entry claiming 'Power rail GNDREF has power_in pins but no power_out or PWR_FLAG'. The PWR_FLAG is present but the analyzer is not recognizing it as satisfying the ERC requirement for GNDREF.

INCORRECT: Q1 (PN2222A) is an NPN switch with R2 (1k) as a collector series resistor (pull-up to the supply) and J3 as the output connector. The collector net (__unnamed_2) has R2 and J3 on it. The analyzer reports load_type as 'connector', ignoring the resistor R2 which is the actual load element on the collector. A more accurate load_type would be 'resistor' since R2 is in series on the collector path. The base_resistors field correctly identifies R1 (10k), but the collector load resistor R2 is not captured.

R2 (1k, at 154.94, 80.01, rotated 180°) is a collector pull-up resistor. Tracing the wires: J2 pin 1 → wire (144.78,72.39)→(154.94,72.39)→(154.94,76.2) → R2 pin 1 (which at 180° rotation sits at 154.94, 76.2). R2 pin 2 (at 180° rotation sits at 154.94, 83.82) → wire → junction at (154.94,87.63) → Q1 collector and J3 pin 2. The analyzer inverts this: it places J2 pin 1 and R2 pin 2 on net __unnamed_0, and Q1 collector with R2 pin 1 on net __unnamed_2. The correct assignments are: __unnamed_0 should contain J2 pin 1 + R2 pin 1 (supply side), and __unnamed_2 should contain Q1 pin 3 (C) + R2 pin 2 + J3 pin 2 (collector/output side). This swap is caused by the analyzer miscomputing pin positions for a 180°-rotated Device:R symbol.
